description: Improve plugin SDK ergonomics: typed prompt args, structured fetch errors, ToolError factory codes, auto displayName/icon defaults, indeterminate progress, internal API cleanup, typed getPageGlobal, storage removal utils, resource schemas, prompt literal types, fetchJSON validation.

userStories.title: Add Zod-based typed arguments to definePrompt
userStories.description: As a plugin developer, I want definePrompt to use Zod schemas for argument type safety, matching defineTool's pattern.
acceptanceCriteria: definePrompt accepts an optional 'args' field with a z.object() schema (in addition to existing 'arguments' array for metadata)
When 'args' schema is provided, the render function receives a typed object instead of Record<string, string>
The PromptDefinition generic infers the args type from the Zod schema
Existing prompts using 'arguments' array and Record<string, string> continue to work (backward compatible)
The e2e-test plugin's prompt is updated to use the new typed pattern
build.ts generatePromptsManifest extracts argument metadata from the Zod schema when 'args' is provided

userStories.title: Add structured ErrorCategory to fetchFromPage errors
userStories.description: As a plugin developer, I want fetch utility errors to include proper ErrorCategory metadata for consistent structured error handling.
acceptanceCriteria: fetchFromPage timeout errors have category: 'timeout', retryable: true
fetchFromPage HTTP errors (non-ok status) have category: 'internal', retryable: false by default
fetchFromPage HTTP 401/403 errors have category: 'auth', retryable: false
fetchFromPage HTTP 429 errors have category: 'rate_limit', retryable: true, with retryAfterMs from Retry-After header if present
fetchFromPage HTTP 404 errors have category: 'not_found', retryable: false
fetchFromPage network errors have category: 'internal', retryable: true
All ToolError instances thrown by fetch utilities use the ToolError static factory methods
userStories.notes:
Edit platform/plugin-sdk/src/fetch.ts. The fetchFromPage function (lines 22-55) currently creates ToolError instances manually without category metadata. Replace with factory methods:

1. Timeout (AbortError/TimeoutError): `throw ToolError.timeout(...)` instead of `new ToolError(..., 'timeout')`
2. Network error: `throw ToolError.internal(...)` with retryable hint — since internal() sets retryable: false, add a custom call: `new ToolError(msg, 'network_error', { category: 'internal', retryable: true })`
3. HTTP errors: switch on status code:
   - 401/403: `throw ToolError.auth(...)`
   - 404: `throw ToolError.notFound(...)`
   - 429: `throw ToolError.rateLimited(msg, retryAfterMs)` — parse Retry-After header
   - Other: `throw ToolError.internal(...)` or `new ToolError(msg, 'http_error', { category: 'internal' })`

Also update fetchJSON and postJSON if they have their own error handling.

userStories.title: Add custom code parameter to ToolError factory methods
userStories.description: As a plugin developer, I want to use factory methods with custom error codes instead of falling back to the constructor.
acceptanceCriteria: All ToolError factory methods accept an optional second parameter for a custom error code: ToolError.auth('msg', 'custom_code')
When custom code is provided, it overrides the default code (e.g., 'AUTH_ERROR' becomes 'custom_code')
When custom code is omitted, the default code is used (backward compatible)
ToolError.notFound already accepts code as second param — verify consistency

userStories.title: Auto-derive displayName and default icon for tools
userStories.description: As a plugin developer, I want displayName to be auto-derived from the tool name and icon to have a sensible default, reducing boilerplate.
acceptanceCriteria: When displayName is omitted from defineTool, it is auto-derived from name: 'send_message' -> 'Send Message'
When displayName is explicitly provided, the explicit value is used (override)
When icon is omitted from defineTool, it defaults to 'wrench'
When icon is explicitly provided, the explicit value is used (override)
The ToolDefinition interface makes displayName and icon optional
The build command (opentabs-plugin build) applies the defaults when generating tools.json if not present
Existing plugins with explicit displayName and icon continue to work unchanged
userStories.notes:
Two files need changes:

1. platform/plugin-sdk/src/index.ts — ToolDefinition interface (around line 107). Make displayName and icon optional:
```typescript
displayName?: string;
icon?: LucideIconName;
```

2. platform/plugin-tools/src/commands/build.ts — in the tool validation and manifest generation. When a tool has no displayName, derive it: name.split('_').map(w => w[0].toUpperCase() + w.slice(1)).join(' '). When a tool has no icon, default to 'wrench'.

Update validatePlugin to not require displayName and icon. Update generateToolsManifest to apply defaults.

The auto-derivation function: 'send_message' -> 'Send Message', 'get_user_profile' -> 'Get User Profile'. Split on underscore, capitalize first letter of each word, join with spaces.

userStories.title: Support indeterminate progress reporting
userStories.description: As a plugin developer, I want to report progress messages without knowing the total count for indeterminate operations.
acceptanceCriteria: context.reportProgress({ message: 'Processing...' }) works without progress/total fields
When progress and total are omitted, the MCP notification is sent with progress: 0, total: 0 (or -1) to indicate indeterminate
The ProgressOptions type makes progress and total optional
Existing tools that provide progress and total continue to work unchanged

userStories.title: Remove _setLogTransport from public API exports
userStories.description: As a plugin developer, I don't want to see internal functions in my IDE autocomplete.
acceptanceCriteria: _setLogTransport is not exported from @opentabs-dev/plugin-sdk public API
The function is still accessible via globalThis.__openTabs._setLogTransport for the adapter IIFE runtime
Plugin developers importing from '@opentabs-dev/plugin-sdk' do not see _setLogTransport in autocomplete
The build command (opentabs-plugin build) continues to work since it accesses _setLogTransport via globalThis, not import
userStories.notes:
Edit platform/plugin-sdk/src/index.ts. Find the line that re-exports _setLogTransport (around line 255-265 in the log re-exports section). Remove it from the export list.

The _setLogTransport function is registered on globalThis.__openTabs._setLogTransport in platform/plugin-sdk/src/log.ts itself (at the bottom of the file). The adapter IIFE accesses it via globalThis, not via import. So removing it from the public exports has no effect on runtime behavior.

Also check if knip flags it as unused after removal — it might need to be marked differently since it's used via globalThis. If knip flags it, add a @public JSDoc tag or add it to knip's ignore list.

userStories.title: Add typed generic overload to getPageGlobal
userStories.description: As a plugin developer, I want getPageGlobal to support a generic type parameter so I don't need 'as' casts everywhere.
acceptanceCriteria: getPageGlobal<T>(path) returns T | undefined instead of unknown
Existing calls without a type parameter still return unknown (backward compatible)
userStories.notes:
Edit platform/plugin-sdk/src/page-state.ts. Change the function signature from:
```typescript
export const getPageGlobal = (path: string): unknown => {
```
To:
```typescript
export const getPageGlobal = <T = unknown>(path: string): T | undefined => {
```

Change the return type from `unknown` to `T | undefined`. The implementation stays the same — the cast happens at the type level only. The final return should cast: `return current as T | undefined;`

Also update the existing `return undefined` early returns to match the return type.

userStories.title: Add removeLocalStorage and removeSessionStorage utilities
userStories.description: As a plugin developer, I want storage removal utilities that follow the same try-catch pattern as the getters.
acceptanceCriteria: removeLocalStorage(key) removes a key from localStorage, returns void, catches SecurityError
removeSessionStorage(key) removes a key from sessionStorage, returns void, catches SecurityError
Both follow the same try-catch pattern as existing getLocalStorage/getSessionStorage

userStories.title: Fix prompt message literal type inference
userStories.description: As a plugin developer, I want PromptMessage types to infer correctly without needing 'as const' casts.
acceptanceCriteria: PromptMessage role field accepts 'user' | 'assistant' literal union without needing 'as const'
PromptMessage content type field accepts 'text' literal without needing 'as const'
Returning { role: 'user', content: { type: 'text', text: '...' } } from render() type-checks without casts

userStories.title: Add optional Zod schema validation to fetchJSON
userStories.description: As a plugin developer, I want fetchJSON to optionally validate the response against a Zod schema for runtime type safety.
acceptanceCriteria: fetchJSON<T>(url, init?, schema?) accepts an optional third parameter: a Zod schema
When schema is provided, the parsed JSON is validated against it; on failure, throws ToolError.validation
When schema is provided, the return type is inferred from the schema (not the generic T)
When schema is omitted, behavior is unchanged (returns T with unchecked cast, backward compatible)
postJSON gets the same optional schema parameter

userStories.title: Add optional schema to defineResource for content type safety
userStories.description: As a plugin developer, I want defineResource to accept a Zod schema for the resource content, providing type safety on read() return values.
acceptanceCriteria: defineResource accepts an optional 'schema' field with a Zod type for the content
When schema is provided, the read() function return type is typed based on the schema
When schema is omitted, behavior is unchanged (backward compatible)
The schema is for documentation/type-safety purposes — no runtime validation is added in the dispatch path
